events.text: Patient is a 52-year-old White male with a BMI of 28.6. No family history of breast cancer. BRCA2 positive (pathogenic variant).

At age 51, patient noticed a painless right breast lump. Biopsy confirmed invasive ductal carcinoma (8500/3), right breast upper outer quadrant (C50.4), grade 3, ER positive (Allred 6/8), PR negative, HER2 IHC 3+, Ki-67 55%.
At age 51, right breast core biopsy: invasive ductal carcinoma, Nottingham grade 3. ER positive 60%, PR negative (
At age 51, mammogram showed a 2.4 cm irregular mass in the right breast subareolar region extending to the upper outer quadrant. BIRADS 5.
At age 51, CT staging showed right axillary lymphadenopathy (largest 2.1 cm). No distant metastatic disease.
At age 51, stage IIB (T2N1M0) right breast invasive ductal carcinoma, HER2-positive, ER-positive, in a male patient. BRCA2 carrier.
At age 51, received neoadjuvant TCHP (docetaxel, carboplatin, trastuzumab, pertuzumab) for 6 cycles.

At age 51, post-neoadjuvant right mastectomy and axillary dissection: residual 0.8 cm invasive ductal carcinoma with treatment effect. 1 of 14 axillary nodes with residual tumor (2 mm focus). RCB class II.
At age 51, underwent right modified radical mastectomy with level I/II axillary lymph node dissection. Residual disease present.
At age 51, given residual disease after neoadjuvant therapy, received adjuvant T-DM1 for 14 cycles per KATHERINE trial protocol.
At age 52, received adjuvant chest wall and regional nodal radiation, 50 Gy in 25 fractions.
At age 52, initiated tamoxifen 20 mg daily given ER-positive disease and male sex.
At age 51, during neoadjuvant TCHP: grade 2 alopecia, grade 2 nausea, grade 1 thrombocytopenia. LVEF stable at 60%.
At age 52, on adjuvant T-DM1 experienced grade 1 fatigue and grade 1 peripheral neuropathy. Platelets remained above 100,000.
At age 52, completed adjuvant T-DM1 and radiation. Tolerating tamoxifen. ECOG 0. Genetic counseling completed for BRCA2. Screening colonoscopy and prostate exam recommended.
At age 52, surveillance CT chest/abdomen/pelvis showed no evidence of recurrent disease. Post-mastectomy changes noted.

At age 51, tumor genomic profiling: BRCA2 germline pathogenic variant (c.5946delT), ERBB2 amplification, TP53 V157F somatic mutation. TMB 4 mutations/Mb. MSS. Homologous recombination deficiency score elevated.
At age 52, LVEF 58% on completion echocardiogram. Within normal limits. Annual cardiac surveillance recommended.
At age 52, patient counseled on surveillance plan: clinical exam every 3-4 months, annual mammogram of contralateral chest wall, annual MRI given BRCA2 status. Currently no evidence of disease.
At age 52, PET/CT obtained for baseline after completing adjuvant therapy. No FDG-avid disease. Physiologic uptake only.

scenario_blurb: Metastatic HER2-positive breast cancer. IHC 3+ or FISH amplified. Treated with trastuzumab-based regimens (THP, T-DM1, trastuzumab deruxtecan). Include hormone receptor status variation (ER+/HER2+ and ER-/HER2+). Some with brain metastases.
